hooks.text: YOU KEEP WAITING TO FEEL READY
hooks.bridge: Instead of waiting, do something now.

hooks.text: MAYBE LATER IS QUIETLY BECOMING NEVER
hooks.bridge: Do it today instead. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: SAFE HAS STARTED TO FEEL A LOT LIKE STUCK
hooks.bridge: Do one small thing that isn't safe. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: YOU KEEP SAVING THE GOOD STUFF FOR SOMEDAY
hooks.bridge: Use some of it today. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: YOUR WEEKEND NEEDS A BETTER STORY
hooks.bridge: Give it something better. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: YOU'VE THOUGHT ABOUT IT LONG ENOUGH
hooks.bridge: Do one thing instead. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: YOU CAN'T PLAN YOUR WAY OUT OF THIS ONE
hooks.bridge: Pick one thing and start. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: PLAYING IT SAFE IS GETTING OLD
hooks.bridge: Do something different today. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: YOUR PLANS DESERVE MORE THAN A MAYBE
hooks.bridge: Make one plan real. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: DO ONE THING YOU'D NORMALLY TALK YOURSELF OUT OF
hooks.bridge: Do the smaller version today. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: YOU DON'T HAVE TO BE GOOD AT IT YET
hooks.bridge: Be bad at something new. Let's find today's side quest.

hooks.text: YOU DON'T NEED A PLUS-ONE FOR THIS
hooks.bridge: Make one plan without them. Let's find today's side quest.

candidates.title:
TAKE YOURSELF
OUT
candidates.completion: Do one thing this week you would usually save for company: a movie, market, class, or meal.
candidates.narration: Go do something on your own that you'd normally wait for a friend to join. Alone, there's nobody to defer to, so you actually find out what you like instead of just going along with the group.

candidates.title:
GO TO AN EVENT
ON YOUR OWN
candidates.spoken_title: go to an event on your own
candidates.completion: Pick one event and stay for thirty minutes before deciding whether to leave.
candidates.narration: Show up to something on your own. Nobody there is watching you the way you imagine — everyone's too busy managing their own awkwardness to notice yours, which is weirdly freeing once you feel it.

candidates.title:
SEND THE
ACTUAL INVITE
candidates.completion: Text one person a plan with a day, time, and place. No vague maybe sometime.
candidates.narration: Pick one person and actually pin it down. A vague 'we should hang out sometime' feels safe because it can't really be turned down, but that's exactly why it never happens.

candidates.title:
WALK INTO A
NEW PLACE
candidates.spoken_title: walk into a place you have never entered
candidates.completion: Enter one cafe, store, class, or bar you have walked past before. Ask one real question.
candidates.narration: Walk into the place you always pass but never enter. The story you've built about it — too cool, not for you, weird to go in alone — almost never survives you actually stepping inside.

candidates.title:
BOOK THE
BEGINNER CLASS
candidates.completion: Find one beginner class and reserve a spot before you can research your way out of it.
candidates.narration: Sign up for something you'll be genuinely bad at. Being a beginner in a room full of strangers is a rep you almost never get as an adult, and it makes the next unfamiliar thing far easier to walk into.

candidates.title:
ASK THE
QUESTION
candidates.completion: Ask someone one question you usually keep in your head because it might sound too curious.
candidates.narration: Ask the question you'd normally swallow. People like being asked about themselves far more than they like being impressed — real curiosity reads as warmth, not nosiness.

candidates.title:
SIT AT THE
BAR
candidates.completion: Have one drink or snack at the bar instead of taking the safest empty table in the corner.
candidates.narration: Take a seat at the bar instead of the corner table. You don't have to talk to anyone — but the corner is a habit, and the bar is where the unplanned, interesting stuff tends to find you.

candidates.title:
SAY YES TO
THE INVITATION
candidates.spoken_title: say yes to the invitation you almost declined
candidates.completion: Accept one safe, manageable invitation you would normally decline out of habit.
candidates.narration: Say yes to something you'd reflexively turn down. Most of your no's aren't real decisions, they're autopilot — and a more interesting month is usually hiding behind one of them.

candidates.title:
FOLLOW THE
FLYER
candidates.completion: The next time you see an offline poster, flyer, or window listing, look it up and commit: RSVP, buy a ticket, or put it in your calendar.
candidates.narration: Pick one thing you almost walked past and follow it through. A good week can start with a real-world invitation, if you let your surroundings surprise you instead of waiting for your usual feed to decide what happens.
footage_queries: person looking at event poster city

candidates.title:
JOIN THE
GAME
candidates.completion: Find an activity already happening — trivia, pickup sport, a group walk, board games, or an open studio — and join for thirty minutes.
candidates.narration: Walk into something already underway and ask to join. It feels like you're interrupting, but open activities only work because new people keep showing up. The awkward part is usually the door, not the room.

candidates.title:
DO THE
TOURIST THING
candidates.completion: Pick one thing people visit your city for and do it this week, even if it feels obvious.
candidates.narration: Go do the thing visitors always do in your city. Familiar places disappear when you only move through them with an errand. Choose one on purpose and your own neighborhood stops feeling like a corridor.

narration.cta_line: Comment done when you pull it off, and follow — there's a new side quest every day.
